content:
# Plan and Implement

## Change Summary

Implemented the remaining prompt-contract hardening for bead `ralph-burning-9ni.6.1`. I updated `validate_canonical_prompt_shape()` so a top-level contract marker no longer emits the misleading "must appear before the canonical section block" error when early canonical sections are simply missing; placement now keys off whether the marker appears after the first canonical heading outside fenced blocks. I also tightened canonical-heading detection so markdown-valid headings with 1-3 leading spaces are treated as contract drift, while `render_bead_task_prompt()` now escapes embedded heading-like prose with 4-space indentation so generated prompts stay markdown-safe. Separately, I changed embedded `Non-goals` and `Acceptance Criteria` extraction to preserve indentation context: only flush-left list markers start new top-level items, so indented sub-bullets stay attached to their parent scope item instead of being flattened into separate bullets. Deterministic regressions were added for all three paths in prompt rendering, contract validation, and bead-backed project creation.

## Steps

1. [Completed] Inspect the current prompt-contract validator, canonical prompt renderer, and section-item extraction logic against the three pending amendments.
2. [Completed] Patch canonical prompt validation to remove misleading marker-placement noise for missing early sections, reject markdown-valid indented canonical headings, and strengthen renderer escaping for embedded heading-like body lines.
3. [Completed] Preserve nested indentation when extracting embedded `Non-goals` and `Acceptance Criteria` items, add deterministic regressions for rendering/validation/create-from-bead behavior, and run focused formatting/tests.

## Validation Evidence

- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo fmt --all` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo fmt --all --check` successfully.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test task_prompt_contract -- --nocapture` successfully, including the new missing-early-sections and markdown-valid indented-heading regressions plus existing stage/panel consumer guidance coverage.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test render_bead_task_prompt -- --nocapture` successfully, including the new nested-list-preservation regression and the updated 4-space heading-escaping coverage.
- Ran `TMPDIR=$PWD/.tmp cargo test create_project_from_bead_context -- --nocapture` successfully, including the new nested-list-preservation bootstrap regression and the updated multiline canonical-field expectations.

## Outstanding Risks

- The bead-description parser still relies on explicit `Non-goals` / `Acceptance Criteria` markers and now intentionally treats only flush-left list markers as new top-level extracted items; if future bead descriptions use materially different prose layouts or intentionally indented top-level bullets, they may remain literal multiline text until the parser is extended.
- I ran focused formatting and regression coverage for the prompt-contract and bead-backed project-creation paths, not the full repository test suite.
- The workspace still contains the pre-existing untracked `.ralph-burning/` directory, which I left untouched.
